mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment growth supports economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window, or the correlation turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a critical threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Central bank tightening typically impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5% for at least two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales (RSAFS) growth and very low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates robust consumer demand and purchasing power, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer health is a key driver of economic growth.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE), even from a low base, signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as corporate earnings decline over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment often precedes economic downturns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of periods following a 0.3pp rise in UNRATE over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer and business confidence, driving demand for related industries and contributing to overall economic growth, which supports equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market strength is a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods when HOUST is consistently above 1200.0.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing and mining activity, reflecting healthy business investment and consumer demand for goods, which is positive for corporate earnings and stock prices over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial output reflects the health of the production side of the economy.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL), especially when accompanied by rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS), erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to a negative outlook for equities as discount rates rise and real earnings fall over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation and rising rates are typically headwinds for equities.
regime_hint: inflationary environment with tightening monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both inflation and FEDFUNDS rise significantly as per the trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and sustained GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a healthy overall economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate profits and thus supports higher equity valuations over the medium term, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is the broadest measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 35% of periods when GDPC1 annual growth consistently exceeds 2.0%.
